claim_boundary: Ticket 30 falsifies a bounded family of simple Collatz frontier potentials and refines the next proof target. It does not prove or disprove RH, Collatz, Goldbach, or Twin Prime.

attempts.attempt: Carry forward the Ticket 29 trichotomy result while preserving the proof boundary: finite stress has not produced a proof or certified counterexample.

source_bounded_result_summary.direct_counterexample_route: No certified off-critical zeta zero was produced.
source_bounded_result_summary.no_counterexample_route: A full proof still requires a tail-uniform all-zero theorem.
source_bounded_result_summary.contrapositive_route: An off-critical zero must imply a bounded failed positivity witness; that bridge is open.

attempts.obstruction: Finite Mertens stress remains compatible with RH and cannot decide the zeta zero set.
attempts.candidate_theorem: A formal tail bridge maps any off-critical zero to a finite positivity violation while controlling every remaining zero contribution.
attempts.next_experiment: Search admissible symbolic tail majorants rather than larger finite Mertens scans.
attempts.claim_boundary: No RH proof and no certified RH counterexample.

attempts.attempt: Try to synthesize the well-founded valuation-debt potential required by Ticket 29. The test asks whether a scalar linear potential over natural exact-cylinder features strictly decreases from every needs_split parent to every needs_split child in the adaptive frontier.

potential_cegis.interpretation: Natural scalar linear potentials over debt, prefix length, consumed bits, and next valuation have many open-child nondecrease counterexamples. The next Collatz attempt needs a nonlinear, lexicographic, or piecewise potential, or a different exact-cylinder invariant.

bounded_result.direct_counterexample_route: No Collatz divergent orbit or non-trivial positive cycle was produced.
bounded_result.no_counterexample_route: The tested scalar linear potentials fail to prove termination of the open frontier.
bounded_result.contrapositive_route: A minimal counterexample would have to evade every all-lift descent cylinder and remain in a frontier where the tested scalar potentials do not decrease.
bounded_result.strongest_closed_statement: The tested scalar linear potential family is falsified on the bounded adaptive frontier.

attempts.obstruction: Scalar linear valuation-debt potentials are too weak for the observed open-child frontier transitions.
attempts.candidate_theorem: A lexicographic or piecewise nonlinear potential over exact 2-adic cylinders strictly decreases after a bounded refinement window.
attempts.next_experiment: Search lexicographic and piecewise potentials on the first violation families from Ticket 30.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Collatz proof, no divergent orbit, and no non-trivial positive integer cycle found.

attempts.attempt: Carry forward the Ticket 29 trichotomy result while preserving the proof boundary: finite stress has not produced a proof or certified counterexample.

source_bounded_result_summary.direct_counterexample_route: No Goldbach counterexample was found in the finite scan.
source_bounded_result_summary.no_counterexample_route: The finite base must be paired with a positive representation lower bound above the cutoff.
source_bounded_result_summary.contrapositive_route: A least counterexample N must be forced below the verified base by an explicit tail theorem.

attempts.obstruction: The explicit large-even lower-bound theorem remains missing.
attempts.candidate_theorem: For every even n>N0, the binary Goldbach representation count is positive, and N0<=5,000,000.
attempts.next_experiment: Name every constant in a main-term-minus-error ledger and reject ineffective exceptional-zero dependencies.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Goldbach proof and no Goldbach counterexample found up to the finite scan limit.

attempts.attempt: Carry forward the Ticket 29 trichotomy result while preserving the proof boundary: finite stress has not produced a proof or certified counterexample.

source_bounded_result_summary.direct_counterexample_route: No certified largest twin-prime pair was produced.
source_bounded_result_summary.no_counterexample_route: Infinitude still requires an exact-gap-2 lower bound on arbitrarily large scales.
source_bounded_result_summary.contrapositive_route: Assume a largest pair; force exact-gap-2 mass beyond it. The forcing theorem is open.

attempts.obstruction: Finite exact-gap persistence does not prove infinitely many exact gap-2 pairs.
attempts.candidate_theorem: A deletion-sensitive exact-gap-2 lower-bound functional remains positive on unbounded intervals.
attempts.next_experiment: Search deletion-sensitive exact-gap functionals that cannot be satisfied by bounded-gap-only evidence.
attempts.claim_boundary: No Twin Prime proof and no certified largest twin-prime pair.
